blocks.body: Some / Any helps you work with indefinite people, places, and things in statements, questions, and negatives.

blocks.heading: Core idea
blocks.body:
• This branch uses Some / Any across three familiar areas: places, people, and things.
  • Some is common in affirmative statements, any is typical in neutral questions and negatives, and no- forms give the negative meaning directly.

blocks.heading: Statements with some
blocks.body:
• In affirmative statements you often see forms such as somewhere, some people, and something.
  • In questions, some usually suggests an expected positive answer or a more specific idea.

blocks.heading: Questions and negatives
blocks.body:
• In neutral questions and negatives, forms with any are usually the safer choice: anywhere, any people, anything.
  • Forms such as nowhere, nobody, and nothing already contain the negative meaning, so they do not need an extra not.

blocks.heading: Pages in this section
blocks.body:
PageLevelFocus
Some / Any — PlacesA2somewhere, anywhere, nowhere, everywhere for place
Some / Any — PeopleA2some people, any people, nobody / no one
Some / Any — ThingsA2something, anything, nothing and patterns with things

blocks.heading: Tips
blocks.body:
🧠

Start with Places if you want the quickest introduction to the core some / any contrast.

Then move to People and Things so the same logic stays clear with somebody / anybody / nothing and similar forms.
